current_task.scope: Fix the installer so @selfdex appears as a Codex skill mention instead of only leaving file-search results.
current_task.verification_target: installer dry run, setup/plugin tests, doctor against Codex home, local skill install, npm package dry run, diff check

orchestration_profile.trigger_basis: After installing into Codex home, the user showed @ mention results containing only Selfdex repo skills and file matches; the @selfdex command-center entry is not exposed as a plugin mention.
orchestration_profile.subagent_permission: @selfdex invocation is explicit standing permission for Selfdex to recommend and use Codex native subagents when useful.
subagent_limits: Read-only explorer, reviewer, docs_researcher, CI/log analysis, and summarization lanes may be used automatically after @selfdex is invoked.
Write-capable worker lanes require a frozen contract and disjoint write boundary.
Commit, push, publish, deploy, production writes, secret access, destructive filesystem operations, and destructive Git operations still require separate explicit approval.
orchestration_profile.selection_reason: This is a focused installer and doctor fix for the @ mention surface; no subagent split is needed.

contract_freeze: npx selfdex install must install both the plugin package and a global selfdex skill into the Codex discovery home.
The @ mention surface may show Selfdex under Skills rather than Plugins; file-only matches are not a successful install signal.
selfdex doctor must fail if the global skills/selfdex/SKILL.md entry is missing.
Manual --home remains available for explicit alternate Codex plugin/skill roots.
Since selfdex@0.1.1 is already published, any npm release for this fix must use selfdex@0.1.2.
No commit, push, npm publish, deploy, secret access, or destructive action is part of this fix unless separately requested.

reviewer.reviewer_target: installer copy behavior, doctor detection, public install wording, npm package surface
reviewer.reviewer_focus: Does npx install create a Codex-discoverable Selfdex skill, and does doctor catch the missing-skill failure mode?
reviewer.reviewer_result: Local diff review found one state mismatch: Current Task phase still said implementation while verification was already local_verified. Fixed in STATE.md. No blocking issues remain in the focused installer surface.

last_update.status: Installer now installs a global Selfdex skill for the @ mention surface, local Codex-home skill install was repaired, and focused verification passed.
verification_result: python -m unittest tests.test_install_selfdex_plugin tests.test_selfdex_setup: pass, 15 tests after sandbox-escalated rerun for Windows temp fixture writes
node bin/selfdex.js doctor --install-root . --format markdown: pass; selfdex-global-skill pass
git diff --check: pass with CRLF warnings only

retrospective.actual_agent_usage: main implementation and local diff review; no subagents spawned for this focused installer fix.
retrospective.next_gate_adjustment: Doctor must keep treating missing global skills/selfdex/SKILL.md as a blocking setup issue because the UI may otherwise fall back to file search.
